task_completion.visual_evidence: The Zotero main application window is visible on the screen.
A Zotero 'Import' dialog box or a file picker dialog launched from Zotero is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The correct BibTeX file is selected for import
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file picker shows 'classic_papers.bib' selected, or the path '/home/ga/Documents/classic_papers.bib' is visible in the import prompt.

task_completion.description: Zotero library is populated with the imported items
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Zotero library view displays a list of newly added items (approximately 10 entries).

task_completion.description: The imported items include the specific expected authors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Creator' or 'Author' column in the Zotero library list clearly shows the names Einstein, Turing, Knuth, and Shannon.

integrity.description: Agent used the Zotero GUI to perform the import
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Zotero graphical interface being interacted with (menus, dialogs), rather than terminal commands modifying the Zotero SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not just open the BibTeX file in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The final result must be shown inside the Zotero application's item list, not as raw text inside an editor like gedit, nano, or VS Code.

integrity.description: Items appear to be genuinely imported rather than manually created
integrity.visual_evidence: The items in Zotero should have complete metadata (titles, years, item types like 'Journal Article' or 'Book') typical of a BibTeX import, not just manually typed titles with missing fields.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is located at /home/ga/Documents/classic_papers.bib. A successful import will result in exactly 10 items added to the Zotero library (acceptable range 9-11). The imported items must include works by the following authors: Einstein, Turing, Knuth, and Shannon.
